title: alnus maritima subsp. metoporina
intro: Alnus maritima subsp. metoporina, also known as the coastal alder, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory abilities, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies for cardiovascular health. Alnus maritima subsp. metoporina can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
